arguments.name: blazor-preview
arguments.prompt:
The user wants to see exactly what files would be generated if they create a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. They do NOT want the project actually created yet — just a dry-run preview showing the file list/tree.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with `--dry-run` to show what would be generated. The key parameters:
- Name: MyBlazorApp
- Output: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server (use `--interactivity Server`)

Run the dry-run command and report the full output so the user can see every file that would be created.

data.content:
Here's the complete dry-run output. **Nothing was written to disk** — this is purely a preview. The template would generate **68 files** organized as follows:

### 📁 Project Structure

| Directory | Files | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `test-output/` | `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `Program.cs` | Project root — entry point & project file |
| `Properties/` | `launchSettings.json` | Dev server launch profiles |
| `appsettings*` | `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json` | Configuration |
| **Components/** | `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor` | Root Blazor component, routing, global usings |
| **Components/Layout/** | `MainLayout.razor` + `.css`, `NavMenu.razor` + `.css`, `ReconnectModal.razor` + `.css` + `.js` | App shell layout, navigation, and SignalR reconnect UI |
| **Components/Pages/** | `Counter.razor`, `Home.razor`, `Weather.razor`, `Error.razor`, `NotFound.razor` | Sample interactive pages |
| **wwwroot/** | `app.css`, `favicon.png` | Static assets & global styles |
| **wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/** | 44 files (CSS + JS, minified, RTL, source maps) | Bootstrap framework (bundled via LibMan) |

### 🔑 Key observations for Server interactivity mode

- **`ReconnectModal.razor`** — present because Server rendering uses a persistent SignalR connection; this component handles reconnection UI when the circuit drops.
- **`Counter.razor`** — the classic interactive counter demo that will use `@rendermode InteractiveServer`.
- **No WebAssembly/WASM files** — since you chose `Server` mode, there's no separate client project or `_framework` output.

### ⚡ Post-creation action

The template would also automatically run `dotnet restore` to pull NuGet packages.

---

When you're ready to actually create the project, just say the word and I'll run the same command without `--dry-run`.

data.content:
Here's the dry-run preview — **nothing was created on disk**.

The `dotnet new blazor -n MyBlazorApp -o ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run` would generate **~68 files**:

```
test-output/
├── MyBlazorApp.csproj
├── Program.cs
├── Properties/launchSettings.json
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
├── Components/
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor (+.css)
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor (+.css)
│   │   └── ReconnectModal.razor (+.css, +.js)
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Counter.razor
│       ├── Home.razor
│       ├── Weather.razor
│       ├── Error.razor
│       └── NotFound.razor
└── wwwroot/
    ├── app.css
    ├── favicon.png
    └── lib/bootstrap/ (44 files — CSS, JS, maps)
```

**Key notes for Server interactivity:**
- **ReconnectModal** is included (handles SignalR circuit drops)
- **No WebAssembly/WASM** client project — everything runs server-side
- A `dotnet restore` would run automatically after creation

Say the word when you're ready to create it for real.
